thoughts: User wants me to run the GChat skill with a dynamic DAYS calculation based on today's weekday.
Today is 2026-04-02, which I need to determine the weekday for.
According to the rules: Tuesday-Saturday=2, Sunday=3, Monday=4
I'll write a quick Python script to calculate DAYS and then execute the gchat_reader.py script with that value.
After starting the long-running task, I need to monitor it every 30 seconds up to 10 times (5-15 min total).
On completion/failure, send appropriate notifications using the notify-user skill.
kvps.headline: Calculating DAYS based on weekday and running GChat sync

tool_args.code:
from datetime import datetime
today = datetime(2026, 4, 2) # 2026-04-02
weekday = today.weekday() # Monday=0, Sunday=6
print(f"Weekday: {today.strftime('%A')} (index={weekday})")
if weekday in [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]: # Tuesday-Saturday
    DAYS = 2
elif weekday == 6: # Sunday
    DAYS = 3
else: # Monday
    DAYS = 4
print(f"DAYS={DAYS}")

logs.heading: A0: Starting GChat sync with monitoring

thoughts: The previous command might have left the terminal session busy. I need to check if gchat_reader.py is already running or start fresh.
I'll kill any existing Python processes related to gchat, then run the script in background with proper logging.
After starting, I'll set up monitoring every 30 seconds for up to 10 iterations (5-15 min total).
The Chrome Devtools endpoint is at 192.168.1.11:9223.
kvps.headline: Starting GChat sync with monitoring

tool_args.code:
# Kill any existing gchat processes
pkill -f gchat_reader.py 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# Start GChat sync in background with logging
nohup python /a0/usr/skills/gchat/scripts/gchat_reader.py --days 2 --chrome-devtools 192.168.1.11:9223 > /tmp/gchat-run.log 2>&1 &
GCHAT_PID=$!
echo "Started GChat sync with PID: $GCHAT_PID"
echo "Monitoring log at /a0/usr/workdir/gchat-debug.log"

# Show initial status
tail -5 /a0/usr/workdir/gchat-debug.log 2>/dev/null || echo "Log not yet available"
